info.title: Omaya call-service
info.description:
Voice runtime for the Omaya maternal-care platform. Bridges Twilio Media Streams to the xAI Voice Agent realtime WSS API so an AI nurse ("Oma") can talk to mothers on the phone. Outbound calls only in Phase 1.

**Job-queue consumer model.** This service does NOT expose a REST endpoint to place calls. At boot the lifespan handler starts a background `job_consumer` task that polls the Postgres `job_queue` table for `call_job` rows (written by the backend's manual-trigger endpoint or the scheduler cron). Each row is dispatched to Twilio via the REST API, and Twilio is told to call back into the HTTP + WebSocket endpoints documented here.

**Current surface (Phase 1 complete).** HTTP endpoints Twilio hits during a call (`GET/POST /twiml/voice`, `POST /twiml/status`), the Railway liveness probe (`GET /health`), and the dev/ops pre-call check (`GET /preflight`). The `/twiml/ws` Media Streams handler and the dev `/dev/voice-test/ws` browser-mic bridge are mounted but absent from this schema — FastAPI does not document WebSockets. See `app/routers/twiml.py` and `app/routers/dev_voice_test.py` for the wire-protocol contracts.

**Phase 4 note.** The SMS pipeline + L4 escalation chain ship on the **backend** service (`POST /webhooks/sms/status`, `POST /alerts/{id}/acknowledge`, the `sms-worker` + `escalation-cron` Railway services). The call-service is unchanged in Phase 4. Provider deliverability issues observed during a call (Twilio Media Streams disconnects) remain a Phase 5+ concern handled here.

**Auth model.** Heterogeneous and route-specific (no global JWT scheme — this service speaks to Twilio and to itself, not to clinicians):
  * `/twiml/voice` and `/twiml/status` — `X-Twilio-Signature` validation against `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`.
  * `/twiml/ws` — secret-bearer token in the URL, set by the signed TwiML response.
  * `/dev/voice-test/ws` — `OMAYA_ENV=dev` mount gate; never reachable in staging or prod.

**Reference page:** `/reference` renders this OpenAPI document via Scalar; `/docs` and `/redoc` are FastAPI's defaults and stay mounted for compatibility.

get.description: Returns 200 while the FastAPI process is serving traffic.

Exercises every external service the call path touches: xAI realtime (WSS handshake), Twilio (creds + number ownership), Supabase Storage + Postgres, and the public tunnel reachable as `CALL_SERVICE_PUBLIC_HOSTNAME`. Returns **200** with `ready=true` if every check passes, **503** with the failed-check details otherwise.

Run before placing a real test call — green here means the call WILL ring (modulo Twilio Geo Permissions, which Twilio does not expose via the API).

post.summary: TwiML response when the mother answers
post.description:
Twilio hits this endpoint immediately after the mother (or her voicemail) picks up. Returns TwiML containing a `<Connect><Stream>` directive pointing at the WebSocket Media Streams handler (`/twiml/ws`) so Twilio will begin pumping μ-law audio frames in both directions.

**Method is POST**, not GET — Twilio's REST API defaults to POST for the answer-URL fetch on outbound calls. The `call_id` query parameter (in the URL query string, not the form body) is the stable UUID assigned at job-enqueue time; it is forwarded into the WSS handler as a `<Parameter>` so the WSS leg can reattach to the `CallSession` registered by the `job_consumer`. If `call_id` is unknown (no in-process state — e.g. the service restarted between dial and answer), the endpoint still returns a polite `<Hangup/>` TwiML rather than 404. Twilio retries 4xx responses, which would ring the mother a second time for a stale call.

**Auth.** `X-Twilio-Signature` validation. Missing or invalid signature → 403. `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN` unset → 500.

200.description: TwiML XML response — either a `<Connect><Stream>` or a `<Hangup/>` for unknown `call_id`.

post.description:
Twilio POSTs lifecycle events here as the call progresses: `initiated`, `ringing`, `answered`, `in-progress`, `completed`, `busy`, `no-answer`, `failed`, `canceled`. Phase 1 only logs the event and (when `call_id` is known) advances the in-process `CallSession` state. No external relay yet.

**Why no relay yet.** Phase 4 will re-introduce an HTTP relay to the backend that drives the alerts dashboard and the SMS escalation pipeline. The parsing helpers (`normalize_status`, `synthesize_failure_reason`) are already in place for that work.

**Form fields.** Twilio sends `CallSid` and `CallStatus` on every event. The optional fields (`CallDuration`, `ErrorCode`, `ErrorMessage`, `SipResponseCode`, `AnsweredBy`) appear conditionally depending on the event type and the StatusCallbackEvent + MachineDetection settings configured on the outbound call.

**Auth.** `X-Twilio-Signature` validation. Missing or invalid signature → 403. `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN` unset → 500. Twilio does not read the response body but expects a 2xx; the JSON body returned here is for operator debugging only.

200.description: Event logged. Body is `{"received": "ok", "status": <normalized>}` for operator debugging.

schema.title: Response Twiml Status Twiml Status Post

tags.name: preflight
tags.description: Dev/ops pre-call dependency check. Verifies the call-service can reach Twilio REST, Supabase Storage, and the configured realtime provider (xAI or OpenAI) before the next job is dequeued. Returns 503 with a per-dependency status map if any check fails. Not part of the production call path — it exists for the runbook in `INFRA.md`.

tags.name: twiml
tags.description: Twilio webhook surface. `GET/POST /twiml/voice` is hit when the mother answers and returns a `<Stream>` directive pointing at this service's `/twiml/ws` WebSocket. `POST /twiml/status` is the call-lifecycle webhook (initiated, ringing, answered, completed, failed). Both are authenticated by `X-Twilio-Signature` validation. The WebSocket bridge itself (`/twiml/ws`) is not in this schema — FastAPI doesn't document WebSockets — and is authenticated by a secret-bearer token in the signed TwiML URL.

tags.name: dev
tags.description: Browser-mic test surface, mounted only when `OMAYA_ENV=dev`. Lets a developer talk to the AI nurse directly through a web page without going through Twilio. Never reachable in staging or prod because the mount itself is gated.
